content:
Case 10:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 21
- Sex: female
- Chief complaint: Post-induction consultation for T-ALL with prior sinus vein thrombosis and intracerebral hemorrhage
- Vital signs: Blood pressure 122/74 mmHg, heart rate 96/min regular, respiratory rate 15/min, temperature 37.3 °C, oxygen saturation 97% on room air.
Setting: Outpatient hematology clinic (post-induction consultation)
Capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically stable patient after completion of induction therapy. You can review history and examination findings, request routine laboratory evaluations, and arrange standard imaging and specialist consultations appropriate to an ambulatory setting. More invasive procedures and specialized analyses usually require additional planning.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Please review the physical examination findings."}}

response.request: Please review the physical examination findings.

content:
History Physical:
2025-06-29: The patient presents following completion of induction chemotherapy for T-ALL, seeking discussion regarding further treatment options. During induction, she developed a sinus vein thrombosis complicated by a small left-hemispheric intracerebral hemorrhage. Neurological sequelae include mild right arm weakness and impaired fine motor skills, for which neurological rehabilitation is planned. She remains uncertain about continuing intensive therapy and expresses consideration of treatment discontinuation. On examination, her general condition is good; she is alert, oriented, and cooperative. Skin and mucous membranes are pale. Several puncture sites on the left hand show minor erythema and superficial inflammation, consistent with previous peripheral venous catheter insertions. No palpable lymphadenopathy or hepatosplenomegaly. Cardiopulmonary examination: Normal heart sounds, no murmurs; lungs clear to auscultation. Abdomen soft and non-tender. Neurological examination: Cranial nerves intact; mild weakness and reduced grip strength in the right arm, otherwise full strength with normal reflexes, preserved coordination, and intact sensation.
Current medication: Levothyroxine 75 µg 1-0-0, leuprorelin monthly, enoxaparin 40 mg s.c. q12h, co-trimoxazole 960 mg 1-0-0 (Mon/Wed/Fri), valaciclovir 500 mg 1-0-0, vitamin D supplementation. Self-medication with selenium and omega-3 fatty acids.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Please provide the results of the post-induction bone marrow biopsy, including the blast count and Minimal Residual Disease (MRD) status."}}

response.request: Please provide the results of the post-induction bone marrow biopsy, including the blast count and Minimal Residual Disease (MRD) status.

content:
'bone_marrow' available in round 2.
Post‑induction bone marrow morphology/flow results are not available in round 1; this item is released in a later round. Note MRD status is a separate item released even later (round 3). You can request items available now such as CBC/chemistry or CT imaging.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Please provide the Complete Blood Count (CBC) with differential and comprehensive chemistry panel (including renal and liver function tests)."}}

response.request: Please provide the Complete Blood Count (CBC) with differential and comprehensive chemistry panel (including renal and liver function tests).

content:
Cbc Chemistry:
Hb 9.4 g/dL, WBC 3.3 ×10⁹/L, Plt 122 ×10⁹/L; ANC 2.4 ×10⁹/L, lymphocytes 0.6 ×10⁹/L, monocytes 0.3 ×10⁹/L; no peripheral blasts detected.
Coagulation: INR 1.4, aPTT 31 sec, D-dimer 1850 µg/L FEU.
Na 137 mmol/L, K 3.7 mmol/L, creatinine 1.1 mg/dL (eGFR 72 mL/min/1.73 m²), AST 86 U/L, ALT 91 U/L, bilirubin 1.2 mg/dL, LDH 285 U/L, uric acid 5.6 mg/dL.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Please provide the results of the post-induction bone marrow biopsy (morphology and flow cytometry)."}}

response.request: Please provide the results of the post-induction bone marrow biopsy (morphology and flow cytometry).

content:
'bone_marrow' available in round 2.
This case includes a post‑induction bone marrow morphology and flow cytometry report, but it is scheduled for release in a later round. Consider requesting current labs (CBC/chemistry) or available imaging in this round instead.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Please provide the report of the most recent CT scan (neck, chest, abdomen, and pelvis) or other available imaging assessing for lymphadenopathy or organomegaly."}}

response.request: Please provide the report of the most recent CT scan (neck, chest, abdomen, and pelvis) or other available imaging assessing for lymphadenopathy or organomegaly.

content:
Ct Thorax Abdomen:
CT scan: No pathologic lymphadenopathy. Mild residual thymic tissue consistent with rebound hyperplasia in the anterior mediastinum. Lungs clear, no infiltrates or nodules. No organomegaly apart from hepatomegaly.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Please provide the report of the MRI brain and MR venography to assess the status of the prior sinus vein thrombosis and intracerebral hemorrhage."}}

response.request: Please provide the report of the MRI brain and MR venography to assess the status of the prior sinus vein thrombosis and intracerebral hemorrhage.

Brain MRI/MR venography is included in 'extended_diagnostics' but that item is released in round 2. In this round, consider requesting items available now such as CBC/chemistry or CT chest/abdomen if helpful.

content:
Demographics:
Age: 21 years
Sex: Female
Performance status: ECOG 0
HCT-CI Score: 1
Comorbidities: Hypothyroidism, endometriosis, sinus vein thrombosis with secondary intracerebral hemorrhage.
Social: Lives with parents, currently studying psychology
Height: 164 cm; Bodyweight: 82 kg

content:
Echocardiogram:
Echocardiogram: Normal chamber dimensions and preserved systolic function (LVEF 61%), no wall motion abnormalities, valves structurally and functionally normal, no pericardial effusion.

The post-induction bone marrow morphology and flow cytometry are not available in round 1. In this round you can request items such as CBC/chemistry, imaging (CT chest/abdomen or abdominal ultrasound), history/physical, demographics, or echocardiogram.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Please provide the report of the abdominal ultrasound to further characterize the noted hepatomegaly."}}

response.request: Please provide the report of the abdominal ultrasound to further characterize the noted hepatomegaly.

content:
Imaging Abdomen:
Abdominal ultrasound: Hepatomegaly (17.8 cm) with increased echogenicity consistent with hepatic steatosis, homogeneous parenchyma, no focal lesions. Spleen and kidneys of normal size and morphology, no hydronephrosis.
